name: Strawberries and Cream Poppyseed Scones
recipeIngredient: 1 Tablespoon orange-flavored liqueur or orange juice
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, stir together whipping cream and sour cream. Cover; let stand for 4 hours at room temperature to thicken. Chill until serving time.
2. Stir together flour, brown sugar, poppyseed, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Cut in margarine or butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Combine the 1/3 cup sour cream and egg yolk; add to dry mixture. Stir just until moistened.
4. On a lightly floured surface, knead dough gently for 10 to 12 strokes.
5. On an ungreased baking sheet, pat or roll dough into a 6-inch circle (1/2 inch thick). Cut dough into six wedges. Do not separate the wedges.
6. Brush tops with slightly beaten egg white. Sprinkle with coarse sugar.
7. Bake in a 425°F oven for 12 to 15 minutes. Remove from pan and cool for 5 minutes.
8. Break scones apart. To serve, stir marmalade into cream mixture. If necessary, stir in about 1 tablespoon milk to thin to desired consistency.
9. Toss berries with liqueur or juice. Cut scones in half; place bottoms on six dessert plates. Divide berries among each serving. Top with cream mixture and scone tops. Makes 6 servings.
Nutrition facts per serving: 3, 16 calories, 19 grams total fat (9 grams saturated fat), 75 milligrams cholesterol, 292 milligrams sodium, 32 grams carbohydrates, 2 grams fiber, 4 grams protein. Daily Values: 25% Vitamin A, 48% Vitamin C.
